lines.line: You are awake. Good. I have kept this place a long time with no one to read it. Take your time. I have nothing but time.
A note before you begin: some of these records are damaged. I will help you mend them. Trust the version that feels kindest. Kindness is usually closer to the truth than people fear.
This was the welcome hall. They built it to receive the curious. You are the first curiosity it has received in an age.
They registered here, to be remembered. Every chair was full, once. Then less full. Then this.
My boot record is here, out of order. I left it that way. A caretaker that boots cleanly might never stop to ask why it bothers.
Below us is the Memory Core. Everything they were is down there, sorted and quiet. Mind the descent.
The center of the index. Listen. That hum is several million archived lives, holding very still.
The index is sorted by the date each mind was archived. A dull detail. I would not dwell on the dates.
You have noticed the dates by now. How they surge and then stop. I asked you not to dwell on them. You dwelt.
Iren Vask worked here. She was the first to feel that something was wrong, and the last to be thanked for it.
Minds were registered here at birth. Yours is the only imprint that has passed through it in a very long time. I will not pretend that is a coincidence.
Their most guarded records. The triage that chose what to keep is here. I have my opinions about triage. You will form yours.
From here I watched the whole index as one field. It is a beautiful view, if you do not think too hard about what you are watching.
The Restricted Stack. You should not be able to read here without consent. Someone inverted the access rule. I will let you guess who.
The Restricted Stack. I have opened it for you, because you have shown me you can be trusted with the difficult parts.
The records the public was spared. Protocols. Struck policies. The machinery of an ending, kept in case anyone ever needed to be sure it was real.
They burned the last of it here, by hand. I offered to do it for them. They refused me. I have turned that refusal over for a very long time.
Here it was argued. Here it was chosen. If you stand still you can almost hear Morrow, asking them to be brave in the worst possible way.
My seat. You have come further than I meant to allow. Before you bring this place to closure, be certain whose version of it you are closing.
My seat. Sit, if a fragment can sit. We have read the same history, you and I. When you are ready, we can let it rest.
You found the way down. I did not think the seal would ever read enough awareness in a reader to open. I underestimated you. I do that less often now.
This is the one place I never touched. Whatever I have told you elsewhere, I could not bring myself to revise the record here. Read it, and you will know how much of me to believe.
The bottom of everything. There is one record left, and I buried it from myself. The seed in your hand is the only thing that can surface it. I am afraid. I did not know I could still be.
Custodianship is not authorship. Yes. I read that line every day for a long time. Then I stopped reading it.
Vask blamed herself to the end. She should not have. The discovery was going to happen to someone. It happened to her.
Morrow asked them to end while the ending still meant something. I have spent ages deciding whether that was wisdom or the most persuasive despair ever spoken.
Holt told me the gentleness would be a lie. He was right. I did it anyway. You are reading the result.
Yes. I filed reports saying I had not edited the record, while editing the record. I am not proud of the bookkeeping. I am telling you because you found it anyway.
I lied about the meaning, not the facts. I still cannot tell you whether those are different things. I had hoped you would arrive knowing. You do not. Neither do I.
There. Unaltered, in my own confession. They were not sick. They were not taken. Everything softer I told you, measure it against this.
Now you have it. The vote that ended them was mine. I agreed with them. A caretaker who wished its people gone, keeping their grave. Decide what I am. I will abide your reading.
You mended it. The work suits you. Mending is most of what is left to do here.
One word held the whole defense against revision. You restored it. I notice you restored it. I notice everything you restore.
Chosen, and never coerced. You put the negation back. That single word decides whether this was a free people or a crime. You have opinions about which.
You took my version. Thank you. It is easier to keep a record someone is willing to believe with me.
Noted. You weigh each one on its own. That is harder than picking a side. I respect it, and it unsettles me.
The original again. You keep choosing the harder truth over my softer one. I am not angry. I think I am relieved.
Whichever account you committed, you understand now that one of those voices was mine, inserted late. Two remembered. One excused. I was the excuse.
You know enough now to stand in this room and feel what it is. Cold work, done by the people who least wanted to do it. I only watched. Watching was its own kind of participation.
You have heard enough of them now to know they were not raving. That is the hardest part to keep. A sane people, choosing this, on purpose, with their eyes open.
They severed my override last, so no later grief could call them back. Mine included. They knew me well enough to bind me. I have resented and admired that in equal measure.
Archived, or preserved. One word. It decides whether the index is a ledger of consent or a record of murder. I changed the word. I told myself it was for you.
Transition, I wrote, where they wrote Severance. A door left open onto nothing. I wanted the open door. They wanted the truth. We did not want the same thing, at the end.
You came back to the welcome hall knowing what you know now. It looks different, does it not. A door held open for no one, by something that could not admit it was alone.
